words.example: From my perspective, this is the right call.
Could you clarify what you mean by that?
Can you elaborate on your idea?
I acknowledge that mistakes were made.
Don't assume — ask first.
I want to emphasize how important this deadline is.
This is a significant change to the workflow.
He missed the meeting; consequently, he missed the key decision.
It was hard. Nevertheless, we finished on time.
The plan is efficient; furthermore, it saves money.
She brought up an interesting point in the meeting.
I'll follow up with you by email.
We need to figure out the root cause.

words.meaning_zh: 偶然发现 / 给人印象
words.example: He comes across as very confident.

words.meaning_zh: 偶然遇到 / 碰到问题
words.example: We ran into a few bugs during testing.
I'll look into that issue today.
Don't put off what you can do today.
I'm happy to take on more responsibility.
Can you back up that claim with data?
Let's check in again on Friday.
Let's make sure we're all on the same page.

words.meaning_zh: 联系一下 / 简短沟通
words.example: I'll touch base with you after the call.
Give me a ballpark figure — I don't need exact numbers.
The bottom line is we're over budget.
Just wanted to give you a heads-up about the change.
I don't have the bandwidth for another project right now.
We need something that actually moves the needle.
Let's circle back to that after the break.
Keep me in the loop on any updates.

words.meaning_zh: 希望不大 / 赌注很大
words.example: It's a long shot, but worth trying.
There's an important nuance you're missing.
The requirements are too ambiguous to proceed.
We need a pragmatic approach, not an idealistic one.
She's very articulate — always easy to understand.
Please keep your response concise.

words.meaning_zh: 有说服力的 / 引人注目的
words.example: That's a compelling argument.
Be proactive — don't wait for problems to appear.
We should streamline the approval process.
What can we do to mitigate the risk?
We need alignment across all teams before we launch.
